author: codex-duplicate-audit

patch.value: Single-source Codex plugin docs tool surface

Duplicate audit 2026-05-22: Codex plugin docs repeat install/tool-surface text with hand-maintained action lists in docs/CODEX_PLUGIN.md:13-33 and plugins/pm-codex/README.md:3-33. Both describe what the plugin provides, install steps, MCP launcher fallback, narrow tools, pm_run actions, and safety guidance, but docs/CODEX_PLUGIN.md:16-17 and plugins/pm-codex/README.md:24-26 differ in named tools/actions. Existing pm-js0r is implementation wrapper/smoke dedup and pm-rjgh does not cover Codex plugin docs.

plugins/pm-codex/README.md or another chosen source is canonical for detailed tool/action inventory; docs/CODEX_PLUGIN.md becomes a short route or generated mirror; listed tools/actions match actual plugin surfaces; docs-skills gate and plugin docs checks pass.

author: codex-dedupe-agent
message: Start docs dedupe for Codex plugin tool surface as part of combined single-source docs PR.

value.author: codex-docs-dedupe-lane
value.text: Evidence from docs/plugin single-source lane (read-only, 2026-05-25): actual Codex plugin inventory from disk is plugins/pm-codex/.codex-plugin/plugin.json + .mcp.json, 3 command prompts (pm-audit, pm-close-task, pm-start-task), 3 skills (pm-auditor, pm-native, pm-release), script launcher, and asset. Actual MCP server source exposes 18 tools: pm_run plus pm_context, pm_search, pm_list, pm_get, pm_create, pm_update, pm_claim, pm_release, pm_close, pm_comments, pm_files, pm_docs, pm_test, pm_validate, pm_health, pm_contracts, pm_plan. Drift found: docs/CODEX_PLUGIN.md lists a narrow "guide" concept but not pm_plan by name; plugins/pm-codex/README.md has the accurate narrow tool names but its pm_run action prose is hand-maintained and omits core actions supported by src/mcp/server.ts (delete, deps, files-discover, config, package/install/upgrade, update-many, history-repair, schema, etc.) while including package-owned dynamic actions. Recommendation: make plugins/pm-codex/README.md the canonical user-facing plugin page; make docs/CODEX_PLUGIN.md a short install/router page that links to ../plugins/pm-codex/README.md and to src/mcp/server.ts or docs/COMMANDS.md#machine-contracts for exact runtime tool/action contracts. Smallest edit: remove the duplicated "What It Provides" inventory from docs/CODEX_PLUGIN.md or replace it with one sentence plus link; in plugin README keep exact narrow tool list, but describe pm_run as "core + dynamically installed package actions; inspect via pm contracts/tools/list" rather than enumerating every action.
